title: Flag surface pinned: 7 flags + auto-detect via [ -t 0 ]
body: API pinned --help/-h, --no-install, --install-theme, --no-theme (wins), --non-interactive, --no-onboard, --force-onboard. Argv parse runs BEFORE Linux guard so --help works on Linux. UX confirmed default-letter convention: [Y/n] for safe, [y/N] for mutating. Integration confirmed propagation via env var (MONSTERFLOW_NON_INTERACTIVE), not argv — survives sub-bash invocations.

title: Exit code matrix: 0/1/2/130 — adds exit 2 for unknown-flag
body: 0=success/bypass/help; 1=REQUIRED-missing/Linux-guard/brew-fail; 2=unknown flag (NEW, distinguishes user-error from REQUIRED-missing); 130=SIGINT cleanup. tests/test-install.sh negative cases N1 (unknown flag → exit 2), N2 (Linux guard → exit 1), N3 (brew-fail → exit 1) lock the contract.

title: Env var contract: MONSTERFLOW_OWNER, MONSTERFLOW_NON_INTERACTIVE, MONSTERFLOW_FORCE_ONBOARD, MONSTERFLOW_INSTALL_TEST, HOMEBREW_NO_AUTO_UPDATE
body: MONSTERFLOW_OWNER extended to accept =0 for adopter-mode test ergonomics (was =1 only). MONSTERFLOW_INSTALL_TEST=1 short-circuits plugin-install + test-suite-validate prompts (resolves Scalability's recursion bug discovery). HOMEBREW_NO_AUTO_UPDATE=1 set unconditionally at top — non-negotiable for the <3s repeat-run budget.

title: Hardened owner detection: realpath + git-rev-parse + env override; security adds $HOME-ownership validation
body: detect_owner() function: MONSTERFLOW_OWNER env override first, else realpath of script_dir compared against `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`. Security agent T3: MONSTERFLOW_OWNER=1 must log loudly + validate $HOME ownership via stat -f %u to prevent hostile parent processes from silently flipping privacy defaults.

title: config/ contents pinned: cmux.json (3-key minimal), tmux.conf (Ctrl-a + cyan/grey 256-color), zsh-prompt-colors.zsh (5 vars + helper)
body: Resolves spec Open Q2. cmux config path verified at ~/.config/cmux/cmux.json via cmux docs. tmux.conf uses ~/.tmux.local.conf source-hook for adopter overrides — no Justin-machine specifics. zsh-prompt-colors.zsh is pure theme: 5 env-overridable color vars + minimal _monsterflow_git_branch helper + two-line prompt. No PATH/alias/plugin behavior.

title: .zshrc sentinel block exact format pinned, source path written via printf %q for safety
body: Format: `# BEGIN MonsterFlow theme` / `[ -f "$path" ] && source "$path"` / `# END MonsterFlow theme`. Security T9 mitigation: source path written via printf %q to handle paths with spaces/specials safely; references the 2026-04-20 PEM-in-.secrets incident. CI grep gate against `curl|wget|eval|bash <(` patterns in config/* files.

title: SIGINT cleanup uses mktemp -d scoped scratch dir, not glob find — Security T6 mitigation
body: Spec's `find $REPO_DIR $CLAUDE_DIR -name '*.monsterflow.tmp' -delete` is too permissive — pre-staged glob attack possible. Replace with INSTALL_SCRATCH=$(mktemp -d -t monsterflow-install); cleanup runs `rm -rf $INSTALL_SCRATCH` only. All atomic writes use $INSTALL_SCRATCH/<name>.tmp then mv -f.

title: Brew-bundle catch pattern: explicit `if !` guard with HOMEBREW_NO_AUTO_UPDATE=1
body: Resolves spec finding sr-pipefail10. Pattern: `if ! HOMEBREW_NO_AUTO_UPDATE=1 brew bundle --file=$REPO_DIR/Brewfile install; then handle_failure; fi`. set -euo pipefail does NOT kill the script when the failing command is the test of an `if`. Failure handler prints message naming common causes (network, broken bottle, locked Cellar), exits 1.

title: gh auth status wrapped with bash trap-alarm 5s timeout (macOS lacks GNU `timeout`)
body: Behind a corporate proxy that swallows port 443, install.sh hangs at the very last step. Bash trap-alarm pattern caps at 5s; if exceeded, skip the auth offer and continue. Mac-portable (no `timeout` binary required).

title: Migration banner pulls version from VERSION file at runtime, not hardcoded string
body: Spec hardcoded 'v0.5.0' in the migration message. Plan: VERSION=$(cat $REPO_DIR/VERSION | tr -d '[:space:]') is already loaded at install.sh:9; reuse $VERSION in the migration message. Survives auto-bump cleanly.

title: Control-flow diff pinned: +180/-8 lines, ~12 edited; deletes lines 82-88 'Continue anyway?' prompt; swaps lines 190-202 owner-detect
body: Single deletion: existing 'Continue anyway?' prompt (lines 82-88) — fully replaced by tier-aware decline. Single swap: owner detection (lines 190-202) → hardened variant preserving OWNER/ADOPTER_ROOT contracts. 8 new stages cut at line-anchored insertion points. Net file ends at ~530 lines (under but close to extract-into-helpers threshold).

title: Three ordering invariants: Linux-guard before path-compute; SIGINT-trap before tmp-write; migration-detect before symlink-mutation
body: Wave 2 strictly sequential to honor these invariants. Subagent gate: dispatch autorun-shell-reviewer against W2 cumulative diff before merge.

title: Test harness: function-shadow has_cmd + MONSTERFLOW_INSTALL_TEST=1 recursion guard
body: Mock strategy: has_cmd() { return 1; } defined in test, export -f has_cmd to inherit. Recursion guard: MONSTERFLOW_INSTALL_TEST=1 short-circuits install.sh's claude-plugins-install prompt + test-suite-validate prompt (which would otherwise re-invoke tests/run-tests.sh and deadlock). Estimated runtime: 4-6s local M-series, ~13s CI — well under 30s/60s budgets.

title: All UX strings pinned in plan/raw/ux.md — box-drawing kept with content-survives-degrade discipline
body: Onboard panel pinned 64x13, contains literal /flow, /spec, dashboard/index.html. Glyph budget: ✓ ✗ ⚠ ○ ⬆ ╭─╮│╰─╯ •. No emoji, no new arrows beyond →. All ⚠ ✗ to stderr. Brew-bundle prompt longest line at 60 chars. Loud notice 3 lines (vs spec's 2) to stay under 80 cols when ≥4 tools missing.

title: scripts/onboard.sh exit code: install.sh treats it fully non-blocking via `|| { stderr breadcrumb }`
body: Onboard failure cannot retroactively fail install.sh — onboarding is operationally separate from installation. Stage 14 invocation: `bash scripts/onboard.sh || { echo '⚠ onboard.sh exited non-zero; re-run anytime via bash scripts/onboard.sh' >&2; }`. Open Q3 in plan: also add `bash -n scripts/onboard.sh` to W3 ship criterion to catch syntax errors at build time.

title: 5 waves: data + flag contract → install.sh stages || onboard.sh → tests → docs
body: W1 (parallel-safe, 6 commits): Brewfile + config/ + flag-parse + python_pip source. W2 (strictly sequential, 9 commits): Linux guard → SIGINT trap → migration → brew REQUIRED → owner-detect → install stage → decline → theme → onboard call. W3 (parallel to W2 once W1 closes, 1 commit): scripts/onboard.sh. W4 (deferred until W2/W3 frozen, 6 sub-commits): tests/test-install.sh. W5 (1-2 commits): docs + BACKLOG cleanup.

title: CHANGELOG.md v0.5.0 bullets must diff-clean against install.sh's print_upgrade_message source
body: Migration message and CHANGELOG are user-facing duplicates of the same fact. Single-source: print_upgrade_message in install.sh; CHANGELOG.md generated from it (or hand-maintained with a CI grep gate that the bullets match).
